planned_changes: Attempt a re-run of the grader/verifier in an environment with openpyxl installed so gate_ringing_selection.xlsx can be parsed programmatically and semantic rubric items (ri_1..ri_4) can be fully verified.

If re-run with openpyxl is not possible, produce and attach to the attempt: (a) a screenshot of the raw_data worksheet showing rows R1..R4 and the global inputs, (b) a screenshot of the method_selection worksheet (or an exported text file) showing the exact cell formulas used for each normalized component, observed_risk_score, margin, and classification, and (c) a short CSV export of the raw_data sheet to show exact numeric inputs.

Update layout_action.md to (a) include explicit cell references for each derived value and formula (e.g., 'sheet RawData!B4 used for R2 vgs_overshoot_V; formula in Methods!C10 = ...'), (b) add one worked example numeric calculation step-by-step for R4 and for one combined-remediation scenario (showing how percent reductions are applied to component risks), and (c) document tie-breaking and boundary-handling rules explicitly.

Correct grader output metadata: remove or set to null any semantic_score or semantic_correctness_score entries that were not programmatically evaluated; if scores must be reported based on markdown-only evidence, mark them as 'provisional' and include a short justification note in metadata.

Revise narrative text to remove claims that the workbook was fully validated unless parsing/verifier checks confirm that; instead state the evidence basis (e.g., 'validated against layout_action.md transcription; workbook parsing unavailable - see attached screenshots').
Re-run the verifier after artifacts are updated and confirm that rubric item scores and metadata are consistent; iterate until verifier issues are cleared.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Artifacts were produced and the markdown transcription (layout_action.md) matches the embedded packet. However, the grader output contains an internal inconsistency: semantic correctness was documented as not programmatically evaluated (openpyxl unavailable) while semantic scores/semantic_correctness_score fields were set to 1.0. The verifier flagged this as an issue and also noted unsupported claims that the workbook had been fully validated. To resolve, either perform programmatic workbook verification (re-run with openpyxl) or explicitly mark semantic scores as provisional and provide workbook screenshots/exports (cell formulas and worked examples).
